job_title: Data Analyst Supporting the US Attorney's Office
strengths: Strong SQL and Python ETL experience
Advanced Excel and Power BI/Tableau reporting
Experience integrating and cleaning multi-source data
weaknesses: No direct criminal justice or probation collaboration
No Lexis-Nexis or credit/BOP report experience
No explicit case opening/lien/demand letter drafting
Background investigation / federal clearance not stated
improvement_bullets: Optimized data operations at Shine Masters by integrating multi-source data (similar to LIONS/PACER processes) and automating case documentation, ensuring high-accuracy reporting and secure handling of sensitive financial client records.

Drafted and maintained complex SOPs for operational workflows, demonstrating the organizational ability to manage case files, generate formal demand documentation, and coordinate cross-functional project updates.

Analyzed large-scale datasets (2M+ rows) to uncover high-risk profiles, utilizing analytical rigor to identify factual patterns similar to reviewing Pre-sentence Reports and credit data for financial litigation.

Collaborated with cross-functional stakeholders including legal and underwriting teams at Haven Technologies to reconcile insurance data, ensuring strict data integrity and compliance with internal documentation standards.

Performed intensive data extraction and validation using Python and SQL to prepare management-ready reports, ensuring the accuracy and timely delivery of information for critical decision-making processes.
cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Analyst Supporting the US Attorney's Office opportunity with FSA. I am interested in this opportunity because of my deep commitment to mission-driven work and my desire to apply my technical analytical skills to support the vital operations of the United States Attorneys Office Financial Litigation Unit. What interests me about working for FSA is its critical role in supporting law enforcement initiatives and ensuring justice is served through effective debt collection and asset recovery, which inspires me because I am eager to use data as a tool for public service and accountability.

I believe my proficiency in large-scale data analysis and my experience in cross-functional stakeholder coordination would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by translating complex data into actionable, secure documentation. In fact, at Shine Masters, I developed interactive dashboards to replace manual reporting, reducing data processing time by 40% while ensuring 100% accuracy in sensitive KPI tracking. This directly resulted in accelerated executive decision-making and improved operational transparency.

Ultimately, I know that the Data Analyst Supporting the US Attorney's Office role at FSA would allow me to bring my experience in database management to the critical responsibility of maintaining case files and generating accurate reports. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my rigorous data cleaning and research skills. I collaborated with underwriting and legal teams to reconcile complex insurance datasets, improving data accuracy by 30%. This project required the same level of attention to detail and ability to handle multi-source data that is essential for processing LIONS and PACER reports at the Financial Litigation Unit.

I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in SQL and Python in working on criminal and civil debt collection reports at FSA. I know that my analytical skills and ability to manage complex project files align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Analyst Supporting the US Attorney's Office role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Warm regards,

Justin Miller
